articles.title: Meet the New England Aquarium’s viral seal and his rubber duckie pal
articles.description:
While the video may look like a simple — and adorable — moment of play, trainers say it also highlights the intelligence and curiosity of harbor seals. 

The post Meet the New England Aquarium’s viral seal and his rubber duckie pal appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: New England Aquarium's harbor seal engages with a rubber duck, showcasing animal intelligence.
The viral video highlights the playful behavior of seals, attracting local visitors.
Increased interest in marine life may boost attendance at the aquarium for Boston residents.

articles.title: What we know about Bravo’s ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’
articles.description:
The show premieres April 2, with the first-ever Fenway Park Bravo night set for April 17.

The post What we know about Bravo’s ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Bravo's 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres on April 2.
Fenway Park will host its first Bravo night on April 17.
Boston residents can expect increased tourism and local engagement from the show's events.

articles.title: 24 Boston businesses approved for new or upgraded liquor licenses
articles.description:
Three pieces of legislation came into play in getting liquor licenses into the hands of restaurants, according to the Licensing Board.

The post 24 Boston businesses approved for new or upgraded liquor licenses appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: 24 Boston businesses received new or upgraded liquor licenses from the Licensing Board.
Legislation changes facilitated the approval process for local restaurants and bars.
Increased liquor licenses may boost local economy and enhance dining experiences in Boston.

articles.title: Mass. man nabbed after allegedly driving over 100 mph in N.H.
articles.description:
Police say the Attleboro man was driving 104 mph in a 55 mph zone on Route 202 near in Rindge, New Hampshire.

The post Mass. man nabbed after allegedly driving over 100 mph in N.H. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Attleboro man arrested for driving 104 mph in a 55 mph zone in Rindge, N.H.
Incident occurred on Route 202, raising concerns about road safety for Boston commuters.
Police emphasize the need for stricter enforcement to prevent dangerous driving in the area.

articles.title: AirTag leads volunteers to hidden cat and six kittens in Waltham
articles.description:
Rescuers from The Cat Connection found the family tucked inside an industrial site and moved them to a foster home.

The post AirTag leads volunteers to hidden cat and six kittens in Waltham appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Volunteers rescued a hidden cat and six kittens in Waltham using an AirTag.
The Cat Connection found the family at an industrial site on March 9, 2026.
The kittens were moved to a foster home, ensuring their safety and care.

articles.title: N.H. Senate approves toll hike for out-of-state drivers
articles.description:
Lawmakers say that more revenue is needed to fund infrastructure improvements, but Gov. Kelly Ayotte is opposing the bill.

The post N.H. Senate approves toll hike for out-of-state drivers appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: N.H. Senate approved a toll hike targeting out-of-state drivers for infrastructure funding.
Governor Kelly Ayotte opposes the bill, potentially affecting future funding decisions.
Boston residents may face increased toll costs when traveling to New Hampshire.

articles.title: One dead after crash in Tewksbury
articles.description:
One person died after two vehicles collided head-on, police said.

The post One dead after crash in Tewksbury appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: One person died in a head-on collision in Tewksbury on March 9.
Police reported two vehicles were involved in the crash.
Local residents may face increased traffic disruptions due to the investigation.

articles.title: Justice Department and Live Nation reach settlement over illegal monopoly case
articles.description:
Ticketmaster and Live Nation Entertainment, based in Beverly Hills, California, have a long history of clashes with major artists and their fans, including Taylor Swift and Bruce Springsteen.

The post Justice Department and Live Nation reach settlement over illegal monopoly case appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Justice Department settles with Live Nation over monopoly allegations affecting ticket sales.
Settlement addresses concerns raised by artists like Taylor Swift and Bruce Springsteen.
Boston residents may see improved ticket access and pricing transparency for concerts.
